enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts all meta-information lines as a set (ignoring order), and the canonical JSON representation of the VCF body is identical between x and T(x)

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Meta-information lines can be in any order, with the exception of ##fileformat which must come first.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair ordering invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the structured meta-line content as a set of key=value pairs (ignoring order within each structured line)

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Left-align indel in homopolymer run preserves variant semantics
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — left-shifting an indel within a homopolymer run produces a canonical representation of the same variant per Tan 2015 §2.1

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

enforced.mr_name: Split multi-allelic record preserves variant set
enforced.oracle: The set of variants represented by the split records (one per ALT) is identical to the original multi-ALT record; Number=A INFO/FORMAT arrays are partitioned per-ALT, GT indices are remapped to preserve REF(0) semantics

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: Fileformat first-line violation must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a VCF file where ##fileformat is not the first line. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: GT allele index out of bounds must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where a GT allele index exceeds the number of ALT alleles. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality violation must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count that does not equal the number of ALT alleles. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column
The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field.

enforced.mr_name: Telomere boundary POS invariance (0 and N+1)
enforced.oracle: Records with POS=0 or POS=N+1 (telomere positions) must survive a write-roundtrip with the same POS value. A parser must not reject, clamp, or convert these boundary positions.

evidence.quote: Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.

enforced.mr_name: VCF-BCF round-trip invariance for codec fidelity
enforced.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and parsing the BCF-round-tripped VCF must yield identical canonical JSON content (all records, INFO, FORMAT, genotypes, and meta-information preserved).

evidence.quote: BCF2 is a binary, compressed equivalent of VCF that can be indexed with tabix and can be efficiently decoded from disk or streams.
In BCF2, the original VCF records are converted to binary and encoded as BGZF blocks.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves variant classification queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — meta-information lines (other than ##fileformat) may appear in any order per spec, so query methods on parsed records must return identical values regardless of meta-line ordering.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field reorder preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured meta-information lines, so query methods must return identical values after reordering key=value pairs.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation with GT remap preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — ALT allele order is semantically meaningful only through its mapping to Number=A and Number=R values and GT indices; when all are consistently permuted, the variant semantics are preserved and query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts INFO field content as a set of key=value pairs (ignoring semicolon-separated ordering)

evidence.quote: Arbitrary keys are permitted, although those listed in Table <ref> and described below are reserved (albeit optional).

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs within a semicolon-separated list have no ordering semantics, but no ordering constraint is imposed either, making this an implicit ordering invariance.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts sample genotype data as a set keyed by sample ID (ignoring column ordering)

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; the invariance is derived from the principle that sample identity is determined by the column header ID, not column position.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with synchronized GT and Number=A/R fields
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the variant semantics (set of alleles and their per-sample genotypes) after normalizing by allele identity rather than position

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column

ambiguity_flags: The spec mandates that Number=A values follow ALT order, so permuting ALT requires synchronizing those values — this MR tests that parsers correctly associate values with allele identity rather than position.

quarantine.mr_name: Trim common affixes preserves variant identity
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the trimmed representation encodes the same biological variant as the original, with POS adjusted by the number of trimmed prefix bases

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means both padded and unpadded forms are valid, making trim_common_affixes a valid normalization invariance MR

quarantine.mr_name: Missing mandatory fixed column must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a data line missing one of the 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The mandatory header line names the 8 fixed, mandatory columns. These columns are as follows: #CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO
Zero length fields are not allowed, a dot (``.'') must be used instead.

quarantine.mr_name: END field coordinate invariance under shuffle_meta_lines
quarantine.oracle: When an END INFO field is present, it must equal POS + len(REF) - 1 (for non-symbolic alleles). After shuffling meta lines and roundtripping, this relationship must still hold.

evidence.quote: This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele
* POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.

ambiguity_flags: END is a computed field and may be absent; the invariant only applies when END is present.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing-value injection invariance for FORMAT fields
quarantine.oracle: Appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing) to all samples must not alter the semantics of existing fields; the canonical JSON for all non-injected fields must remain identical.

evidence.quote: If genotype information is present, then the same types of data must be present for all samples.
In all cases, missing values are specified with a dot (`.').

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'Additional Genotype keys can be defined in the meta-information, however, software support for them is not guaranteed' — some parsers may reject unknown FORMAT keys even if filled with missing values.

quarantine.mr_name: BCF header dictionary permutation round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: Shuffling ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER lines, round-tripping through BCF, and converting back to VCF must yield identical canonical JSON content regardless of dictionary entry ordering.

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The IDX field mechanism allows explicit dictionary positions; if the SUT uses IDX, the round-trip may produce different implicit vs explicit indexing.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves variant queries
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — the INFO column's semicolon-separated key=value pairs have no required ordering, so query methods on parsed records must return identical values regardless of INFO field order.

evidence.quote: Clarified INFO lines field ordering

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs in data lines have no required order, but the clarification of 'INFO lines field ordering' and the general principle that implementations must not rely on field ordering supports this interpretation.

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves variant queries
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — sample column ordering is not semantically significant for per-record query methods; parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

evidence.quote: It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.
It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.
